CheckTitle: Ensure agent identities do not share a certificate credential
Description: Detects the same certificate (identical customKeyIdentifier thumbprint) registered on more than one agent identity or agent blueprint. Shared credentials mean multiple agents authenticate as indistinguishable principals.
Risk: When several agents share one credential, actions cannot be attributed to a specific agent and revoking the credential disrupts every agent that relies on it. A single leaked secret compromises all agents that share it, and accountability for least-privilege enforcement collapses.

Remediation.Recommendation: Issue a unique, dedicated credential (federated identity credential or a distinct certificate) to each agent identity. Remove the shared certificate and rotate to per-agent credentials so every agent authenticates as a distinct, revocable principal.

SourceNotes: Detection compares the customKeyIdentifier (SHA-1 certificate thumbprint) across agent identities and blueprints. Shared client secret VALUES cannot be detected from Graph (secret values are never returned); only shared certificates are observable. Grounded in Microsoft's 'Least privilege for AI agents' guidance (2026): shared secrets across multiple agents erase accountability.
